generalizable_rule: When a modal or dialog is visible, prioritize actions inside the modal before interacting with the background page.
After changing a filter, date, passenger count, or option picker, look for an explicit Apply, Done, Update, Continue, or Search confirmation before moving on.
Prefer semantic clickable ancestors such as buttons or links over decorative child nodes like svg, span, or icon wrappers.
In travel search flows, fill or confirm all required fields such as date, location, guest, or passenger count before triggering the final Search or Continue action.
In checkout or reservation forms, complete required inputs first, then handle confirmations such as Apply, Save, or Continue in the active form section.
When the UI has clearly moved into a new local subflow, prefer actions that continue the active branch instead of jumping back to unrelated controls.
